name: Anasazi-Tofu Enchiladas
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the first five ingredients together.
2. Add 2 tablespoons enchilada sauce.
3. Moisten the tortillas in warmed enchilada sauce.
4. Fill each tortilla with the mixture and roll it up, placing the opening of the roll down.
5. Pour any remaining sauce over the enchiladas once the pan is filled to the top.
6. Place shredded cheeses on top of the enchiladas.
7. Bake at 300 degrees F for 35-40 minutes.
"Anasazi" is a Navajo word meaning "ancient one." Anasazi beans have been found in many southwestern Indian dwellings. Bean connoisseurs will appreciate the characteristic taste and texture of pinto and kidney combined within this ancient Indian entrée. Versatility of this variety is limited only by your imagination: try them for a "dip," use them in creative Mexican food construction, or enjoy them straight!
